articles.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
articles.summary: AI drug discovery startup Converge Bio raised $25 million in a Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with additional backing from executives at Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz.

articles.title: How one AI startup is helping rice farmers battle climate change
articles.summary: Mitti Labs is working with The Nature Conservancy to expand the use of climate-friendly rice farming practices in India. The startup uses its AI to verify reductions in methane emissions.

articles.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
articles.summary: After developing a facial-recognition app for Meta’s Ray-Ban glasses and doxing random people, two former Harvard students are now launching a startup that makes smart glasses with an always-on microphone.

articles.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
articles.summary: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

articles.title: Roundtables: Longevity’s Next Frontier: “Reprogramming” Your Body
articles.summary: Listen to the session or watch below Billions of dollars are flooding into efforts to reverse aging as scientists explore ways to return cells to a younger state. articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. articles.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
articles.summary: Notion is "going all in on using agents to run your inbox."

articles.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
articles.summary: Move originally planned for Monday would have heavily increased power users' costs.

articles.title: Goose, a New Gay Dating App, Appears to Be a Psyop
articles.summary: Touted as a less-hookup-focused Grindr, Goose is an invite-only space for gay men. The problem is the people promoting it don’t seem real.

articles.title: You Can Now Sound the Alarm on AI Behaving Badly
articles.summary: Are you worried your AI chatbot is trying to build a bomb or leak personal information about you? There’s a website for that.

articles.title: Anthropic Added a New Security Measure to Get Back Into the Trump Administration’s Good Graces
articles.summary: The government has removed restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models—but there were strings attached.

articles.title: Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival
articles.summary: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.

articles.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
articles.summary: Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. AI promised to change that. articles.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
articles.summary: Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate 60-second vertical AI clips based on the sources you upload to the app. The example shared by Google detai
